purpose: Machine-readable completion tracker for the coordinated large-push research/performance lanes. Percentages are engineering readiness estimates backed by checked-in evidence, not physics claims by themselves.

lanes.title: Production-resolution QI CPU/GPU robustness

evidence.claim: Production-readiness manifest rolls up all checked QI CPU/GPU seed artifacts and explicitly keeps the gate bounded until production CPU/GPU ladders exist.
New five-seed bounded CPU QI ladder passes seeds 0..4 with zero process failures and maximum residual ratio below 1e-6.
Bounded QI CPU and one-GPU auto policy now converges at the higher scale-0.35 probe after host-sparse rescue.
Runner materializes deterministic QI neighboring decks with residual and acceptance gates.
CI-fast runner tests lock deterministic generation and gate semantics.
New scale-0.45 CPU QI probe passes at 11 x 23 x 45 x 4 in 106.0 s with public auto path and residual ratio below 5e-7.
Scale-0.50 CPU QI probe is checked in as timeout blocker evidence and is excluded from completion estimates.

gates: Residual ratio must remain below the requested gate on CPU and GPU.
Production-resolution promotion still requires wider seeds and wall-time/memory budgets.
No QI-specific default branch may bypass the public auto solver policy.
next_actions: Investigate why scale-0.50 public-auto QI times out after the scale-0.45 CPU success.
Run larger CPU and one-GPU QI seed ladders only after the timeout boundary is reduced or the solver policy is improved.
lanes.second_push_result: bounded CPU probe at scale 0.45 passed; scale 0.50 timed out, so production CPU/GPU remains blocked

lanes.title: PAS and geometry-rich runtime/memory offenders

evidence.claim: Production-floor PAS-TZ tzfft CPU artifact converges at the collaborator floor without dense allocation.
No-op PAS matrix-free corrections now avoid the extra candidate matvec while preserving residual history and reject reason.
Benchmark harness now rejects default-promotion candidates that regress elapsed time or peak RSS against baseline.
Docs now distinguish promoted PAS memory routes from rejected experimental routes.
Structured PAS-TZ guard metadata records production-grid memory rejection estimates without allocating dense guards.
Matrix-free PAS tests cover tiny-update and candidate-size fail-fast gates that avoid wasted candidate matvecs.

gates: Keep only routes that improve runtime or memory while preserving true residual and Fortran parity.
Reject fallback candidates that avoid allocation but do not reduce the residual.
CPU and GPU default policy changes require bounded artifacts before README-facing claims.
next_actions: Run a bounded production geometry-rich solve that exercises the new PAS guard metadata and candidate-size gates.
Promote defaults only after residual, runtime, and peak-RSS gates all improve on geometry-rich CPU and GPU targets.
lanes.second_push_result: bounded PAS fail-fast gates and metadata landed; production residual-clean default-promotion evidence still missing

lanes.title: Executable parallelism and strong-scaling evidence

evidence.claim: Transport-worker benchmark now has a plan-only mode that records deterministic RHS partitioning, skipped worker counts, timing semantics, and release-gate semantics without launching solves.
Single-case sharded-solve benchmark now has a plan-only mode that records timeout, backend, sharding, and non-release scaling semantics before launching expensive samples.
Multi-GPU case-throughput benchmark now has a plan-only mode that records warmup, sequential one-GPU, and parallel two-GPU child process plans before launching GPU work.
Sharded matvec benchmark now records deterministic plan-only metadata for device counts, padding, compiled-hot-loop timing, and memory-gate semantics.
New tests validate capped worker plans, cold-start rejection semantics, and GPU worker schedule coalescing.
New tests keep multi-GPU throughput plans non-release and verify the internal sharded-solve command contract.
New tests validate single-case sharded plan JSON and ensure it remains an experimental non-release claim.
Release-facing GPU transport-worker throughput figure is checked in.
Parallelism docs describe process, worker, GPU, and validation routes.
Runtime policy module owns worker-count and backend decisions.
Pure multi-GPU case-throughput audit rejects cold/mixed timing, non-GPU payloads, release overclaims, and sub-unity speedups.

gates: Case-parallel and transport-worker paths must preserve serial output ordering and parity.
Strong-scaling claims require per-worker work large enough to amortize setup.
Single-case multi-GPU remains experimental unless a measured publication-grade artifact improves wall time.
next_actions: Run the timeout-safe two-GPU throughput command on office and check the throughput audit payload.
Keep single-case strong scaling experimental until a measured warm artifact beats one device.
lanes.second_push_result: timeout-safe two-GPU throughput audit landed; measured release-grade speedup remains missing

lanes.title: Refactor, source map, coverage, and CI readiness

evidence.claim: Dynamic docstring tests now cover public APIs in split policy modules so refactors stay discoverable.
New pure-policy tests improve coverage of transport solver, backend, and parallel environment decisions without launching slow solves.
Source-map docs identify policy, solver, transport, geometry, and validation modules.
Release-claim metadata is enforced without launching expensive solves.
Host refinement logic was split out of the driver and became directly testable.
Fast tests cover measured candidate promotion, memory metric ordering, failed-baseline rescue, and tie-breaking.
Residual-gate tests cover env parsing, negative threshold normalization, nonfinite diagnostics, and array failure collection.

gates: Coverage gains must come from focused module tests, not slow full-solve duplication.
Large driver splits must preserve CLI/API parity and existing solver provenance.
CI-fast checks must remain within the documented time budget.
next_actions: Continue splitting pure solver/path/diagnostic helpers from v3_driver.py with fast focused tests.
Move package coverage to a JAX-safe CI coverage environment before claiming 95%.
lanes.second_push_result: focused solver-policy/residual coverage improved; package-wide 95% remains blocked by broader driver decomposition and JAX-safe coverage

lanes.title: vmec_jax to booz_xform_jax to sfincs_jax differentiable workflow

evidence.claim: New docs page states the skip-safe workflow, commands, differentiability contract, and non-claims.
New status scaffold reports optional backend availability and exact proxy-gradient commands without importing optional packages.
Optional differentiable geometry handoff example exists and is documented.
Finite-beta VMEC-to-SFINCS profile figure is checked in for user-facing docs.
Adapter module isolates JAX-native geometry inputs from file-backed VMEC loading.
Optional workflow tests cover backend readiness and measured adoption summaries without hard dependencies.

gates: End-to-end optimization claims require differentiating through a validated geometry-to-transport scalar.
Boozer and VMEC optional dependencies must fail gracefully when not installed.
Profile-current and ambipolar outputs must pass convergence gates before publication claims.
next_actions: Add a pure-JAX scheme-5 transport-objective gradient gate before claiming full kinetic transport gradients.
Keep VMEC/Boozer optional dependencies skip-safe in default CI.
lanes.second_push_result: target saturated for scoped geometry-proxy workflow; full kinetic transport gradients remain out of scope

lanes.title: JAX ecosystem solver/preconditioner evaluation

evidence.claim: New tests run measured optional Lineax, Equinox, and JAXopt gates while skipping safely if optional packages are absent.
Docs record the memory/runtime rationale for short-recurrence Krylov, checkpointing, and JAX profiling.
Performance-technique docs record keep/reject gates for solver candidates.
Solver-selection policy is isolated enough to host future lineax/equinox experiments behind gates.
Lineax benchmark now writes adoption summaries that separate synthetic speedups from real SFINCS solver-error statuses.
Equinox/JAXopt gate now reports measured directional-gradient and optimization summary decisions.

gates: No new JAX ecosystem dependency is promoted unless it reduces runtime or memory on a documented case.
Any replacement solver must preserve autodiff semantics or explicitly remain in the non-differentiable CLI lane.
Hard-coded branch thresholds should move to measured policy gates when evidence exists.
next_actions: Resolve Lineax real-SFINCS solver error statuses before any solver promotion.
Keep Equinox/JAXopt examples as optional bounded optimization workflows rather than hard dependencies.
lanes.second_push_result: measured Lineax/Equinox/JAXopt adoption summaries landed; Lineax remains unpromoted on real SFINCS error statuses

lanes.title: Benchmark, parity, README, and documentation publication surface

evidence.claim: New CI-fast research-lane checker prevents subjective or unsupported completion claims.
New tests enforce evidence paths, non-regressing completion percentages, and substantial per-lane deltas.
README-facing Fortran/JAX runtime and active-memory comparison figure is checked in.
Validation manifest records release-ready, bounded-proxy, and deferred lanes explicitly.
Benchmark artifact policy prevents stale or inconsistent runtime/memory claims.
New doc-claim tests prevent stale benchmark ratio, figure, and scope wording from shipping.
Benchmark-summary tests check the checked-in report against default regeneration output.

gates: README table and figure must be generated from the same canonical filtered rows.
No jax_error or max_attempts row may appear in public release-facing parity claims.
Low-resolution CI smoke rows must not be promoted into runtime comparison claims.
next_actions: Regenerate final plots only after accepted performance-policy changes alter benchmark data.
Keep README/docs scope language tied to canonical checked benchmark rows.
lanes.second_push_result: target saturated for docs/release consistency gates; no benchmark data regenerated in this push

second_push_assessment: partial: target saturation achieved for VMEC/Boozer and benchmark/docs; QI, PAS, parallel, and refactor gained evidence but not all achieved +15pp from the immediately preceding baseline.
